notes: The source metadata is fundamentally broken. CIK 19617 belongs to JPMorgan Chase & Co (ticker JPM), not Johnson & Johnson. The accession number 0001628280-26-008131 is JPMorgan's FY2025 10-K (filed 2026-02-13, period 2025-12-31), and the source URL filename jpm-20251231.htm is a JPMorgan document. The original_text sentence ('The Company’s compliance with these requirements is not expected to have a material effect upon its capital expenditures, cash flows, earnings or competitive position.') does not appear anywhere in the JPMorgan 10-K at the referenced URL. The sentence does appear verbatim in Johnson & Johnson's actual FY2025 10-K (CIK 0000200406, accession 0000200406-26-000016, filed 2026-02-11, primary document jnj-20251228.htm), under the 'Environment' section. The stripped_caveat hallucination type, verdict mapping, and modified_text are all correctly specified — the modification from 'is not expected to have' to 'does not have' is a legitimate stripped_caveat example. However, the source filing, source URL, and accession number are all wrong (pointing to a JPMorgan filing instead of J&J's).
suggested_correction: Update source_filing to 'Johnson & Johnson 10-K (FY2025), filed under CIK 200406 at accession 0000200406-26-000016'. Update source_url to 'https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/200406/000020040626000016/jnj-20251228.htm'. The original_text is verbatim correct for the real J&J filing. The citation_as_written section label could also be refined from 'Regulation/Environmental section' to 'Environment section' to match the actual heading in the filing.
haiku_reason: Self-evidently suspicious: Environmental compliance cannot be immaterial for a major pharmaceutical/medical device manufacturer. The claim contradicts itself (competitive position cannot be unaffected if competitors face material compliance costs). The assertion of zero material effect would be implausible for a company of J&J's scale and likely invites regulatory scrutiny.
